[Pleidelsheim](/wiki/Pleidelsheim "Pleidelsheim") 18. [Schwieberdingen](/wiki/Schwieberdingen "Schwieberdingen") 19. [Sersheim](/wiki/Sersheim "Sersheim") 20. [Walheim](/wiki/Walheim "Walheim")

is a [*Landkreis* (district)](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") in the middle of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Neighboring districts are (from north clockwise) [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn_(district) "Heilbronn (district)"), [Rems-Murr-Kreis](/wiki/Rems-Murr-Kreis "Rems-Murr-Kreis"), the district-free city [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), and the districts [Böblingen](/wiki/Boeblingen_(district) "Boeblingen (district)") and [Enz-Kreis](/wiki/Enz_(district) "Enz (district)").

["Tabellengruppe 12411: Fortschreibung des Bevölkerungsstandes zum 31. Dezember 2024"](https://daten.statistik-bw.de/genesisonline/online?operation=statistic&levelindex=0&levelid=1757432774350&code=12411#abreadcrumb) [Update of the population as of 31 December 2024] (in German). [Statistisches Landesamt Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Statistisches_Landesamt_Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Statistisches Landesamt Baden-Württemberg").

**Landkreis Ludwigsburg** is a [*Landkreis* (district)](/wiki/Districts_of_Germany "Districts of Germany") in the middle of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Neighboring districts are (from north clockwise) [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn_(district) "Heilbronn (district)"), [Rems-Murr-Kreis](/wiki/Rems-Murr-Kreis "Rems-Murr-Kreis"), the district-free city [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart"), and the districts [Böblingen](/wiki/Boeblingen_(district) "Boeblingen (district)") and [Enz-Kreis](/wiki/Enz_(district) "Enz (district)").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigsburg_(district)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The district dates back to the *Oberamt Ludwigsburg*, which was created by the dukedom [Württemberg](/wiki/W%C3%BCrttemberg "Württemberg") in the beginning of the 19th century. After several small changes during the century, it was converted into a district in 1938. Several municipalities of the dissolved *Oberämter* Besigheim, Marbach and Waiblingen were added to the newly formed district.

As a result of the communal reform of 1973, the district gained about half of the dissolved district [Vaihingen](/wiki/Vaihingen_an_der_Enz "Vaihingen an der Enz"), and some few municipalities from the districts Backnang and Leonberg.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigsburg_(district)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The main river in the district is the [Neckar](/wiki/Neckar "Neckar"), which divides the district into a big western part and a smaller eastern part.

## Partnerships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigsburg_(district)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Partnerships")]

Starting in 1990 the district has a partnership with the district [Chemnitzer Land](/wiki/Chemnitzer_Land "Chemnitzer Land") (now part of the district of [Zwickau](/wiki/Zwickau_(district) "Zwickau (district)")) in [Saxony](/wiki/Saxony "Saxony"). Even older is the partnership with the region [Upper Galilee](/wiki/Upper_Galilee "Upper Galilee") in [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel").
Since 1992 the district has a partnership with the [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") [Komitat Pest](/wiki/Pest_(county) "Pest (county)"), however the official signing of the partnership contract took place in 2002. A friendship exists with the city [Yichang](/wiki/Yichang "Yichang") in the province [Hubei](/wiki/Hubei "Hubei"), [China](/wiki/China "China").

## Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigsburg_(district)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

The imperial eagle in the coat of arms is taken from the arms of the city Markgröningen, which was an imperial city. It was later adopted by the city Ludwigsburg as well. The deer antler above the eagle is the symbol of [Württemberg](/wiki/W%C3%BCrttemberg "Württemberg").
